/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.node.c.description: Monitor RabbitMQ node-level events (node going down, alarms raised) during an experiment. For queue-level monitoring, use Check Queue Backlog instead.
/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.node.c.label: Check Nodes

parameters.description: How long the check runs. Node state is polled continuously for this duration.
parameters.label: Duration
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: Which node-level changes to watch for. If left empty, any node change triggers the check.
parameters.label: Expected Changes
parameters.name: expectedChanges

parameters.description: How expected changes are evaluated. 'All the time': every poll must detect it. 'At least once': observed once during the duration.
parameters.label: Change Check Mode
parameters.name: changeCheckMode

/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..description: Limit the maximum number of messages a queue can hold by creating a temporary RabbitMQ policy. The policy is removed when the attack ends.
/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..label: Alter Queue Max Length

parameters.description: How long the policy stays in effect. The policy is automatically removed when the duration expires.
parameters.label: Duration
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: Maximum number of messages the queue will hold. Messages beyond this limit are dropped or dead-lettered. Set 0 for unlimited.
parameters.label: Max Length
parameters.name: maxLength

/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..description: Monitor the total message count (backlog) in a RabbitMQ queue and fail the experiment if it exceeds a threshold. For node-level monitoring (downtime, alarms), use Check Nodes instead.
/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..label: Check Queue Backlog

parameters.description: How long the check runs. Queue backlog is polled continuously for this duration.
parameters.label: Duration
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: Maximum acceptable message count in the queue. If backlog exceeds this value, the experiment fails.
parameters.label: Backlog alert threshold
parameters.name: acceptableBacklog

/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..description: Publish a fixed total number of messages to a queue, distributed evenly across the duration. For rate-based publishing (messages/second), use Publish (Messages / s) instead.
/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..label: Publish (# of Messages)

parameters.description: Optional. The AMQP exchange to publish to. If empty, messages go directly to the target queue via the default exchange.
parameters.label: Exchange
parameters.name: exchange

parameters.description: Optional routing key used for message delivery; defaults to the queue name if empty.
parameters.label: Routing Key
parameters.name: routingKey

parameters.description: Message headers to include in AMQP properties.
parameters.label: Message Headers
parameters.name: headers

parameters.description: Required. The message body to publish. Every message uses this same value.
parameters.label: Message body
parameters.name: body

parameters.description: Total number of messages to publish across the entire duration. The publishing rate is this value divided by the duration.
parameters.label: Number of Messages
parameters.name: numberOfMessages

parameters.description: How long the publisher runs, in seconds. The total number of messages is distributed evenly across this duration.
parameters.label: Duration (seconds)
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: Maximum number of parallel publish requests in flight at once. Min: 1, Max: 10.
parameters.label: Max concurrent requests
parameters.name: maxConcurrent

/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..description: Publish messages to a queue at a constant rate (messages per second) for the attack duration. For publishing a fixed total number of messages, use Publish (# of Messages) instead.
/com.steadybit.extension_rabbitmq.queue..label: Publish (Messages / s)

parameters.description: Optional. The AMQP exchange to publish to. If empty, messages go directly to the target queue via the default exchange.
parameters.label: Exchange
parameters.name: exchange

parameters.description: Optional routing key used for message delivery; defaults to the queue name if empty.
parameters.label: Routing Key
parameters.name: routingKey

parameters.description: Message headers to include in AMQP properties.
parameters.label: Message Headers
parameters.name: headers

parameters.description: Required. The message body to publish. Every message uses this same value.
parameters.label: Message body
parameters.name: body

parameters.description: The number of messages per second. Should be between 1 and 10.
parameters.label: Messages per second
parameters.name: messagesPerSecond

parameters.description: How long the publisher runs at the specified rate, in seconds. Total messages produced = rate x duration.
parameters.label: Duration (seconds)
parameters.name: duration

parameters.description: Minimum percentage of messages that must be published without errors. If the success rate drops below this, the experiment fails.
parameters.label: Required Success Rate
parameters.name: successRate

parameters.description: Maximum number of parallel publish requests in flight at once. Min: 1, Max: 10.
parameters.label: Max concurrent requests
parameters.name: maxConcurrent
